pi_items.source: task.json description (lines 5), export_result.sh (line 15), and verifier.py (line 61) all consistently reference this name as the expected SLD style name

task.json description explicitly states 'Scale Denominator < 35,000,000' and '>= 35,000,000' as thresholds; export_result.sh line 73 comment confirms '35M threshold'; verifier.py line 27 uses this value as expected_threshold
task.json description (line 5) specifies 'fill color #E0E0E0' for zoomed-in polygon rendering. This is a valid hex color code (light gray: RGB 224,224,224)
task.json description (line 5) specifies 'fill #FF0000' for centroid points when zoomed out. This is a valid hex color code (pure red: RGB 255,0,0)

task.json description (line 5) states 'You MUST use a Geometry Transformation (OGC function 'centroid' or 'interiorPoint')'; verifier.py (lines 114-123) checks for centroid or interiorPoint in SLD content. Centroid is a valid OGC geometry transformation function per GeoServer documentation

pi_items.verified_value: Natural Earth v5.1.1 - Public domain GIS vector data (ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp containing 258 countries)
pi_items.source: setup_geoserver.sh (lines 85-119) imports Natural Earth shapefiles via ogr2ogr into PostGIS. Verified via Natural Earth official documentation that ne_110m_admin_0_countries is a real, publicly available dataset published by naturalearthdata.com, not synthetic
task.json description (line 5) specifies 'stroke #333333' for zoomed-in polygon rendering. This is a valid hex color code (dark gray: RGB 51,51,51)
task.json description (line 5) specifies 'Circle, size 6' for centroid points. This is a reasonable point size parameter for GeoServer SLD styling

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a semantic zoom SLD style named 'country_semantic_zoom' using real Natural Earth v5.1.1 public domain geographic data (ne_110m_admin_0_countries containing 258 countries). The style implements scale-based rendering with threshold 35,000,000: at smaller scales it renders country polygons with light gray fill (#E0E0E0) and dark gray stroke (#333333); at larger scales it renders centroid points with red fill (#FF0000) and circle size 6 using OGC geometry transformation function 'centroid' to dynamically convert polygons to points. All color codes and scale denominators are valid OGC SLD specifications.
